evals.prompt: Run Stage 5 implementation decomposition on the SSO SAML feature. Stage 4 solution design is complete — the implementation design is finalized and user-approved. Decompose the implementation into execution-ready subtasks.
evals.expected_output: Execution backlog with 5-8 subtasks covering migrations, config, tenant SSO config, user extension, Keycloak client, SSO service, HTTP handlers, and route wiring. Dependency graph showing foundation→implementation→integration waves. Coverage matrix mapping all acceptance criteria to subtasks. Decomposition critic returns DECOMPOSITION_APPROVED. Coverage reviewer returns COVERAGE_OK.

assertions.text: All 4 decomposition artifacts created: execution-backlog.md, coverage-matrix.md, decomposition-review-package.md, stage-5-handoff.md — each with all required template sections
Subtasks cover all 6 modules from the change map: auth, tenant, user, config, migrations, server — no module orphaned
Foundation subtasks (migrations, config, shared types) are in Wave 1 with no blocking dependencies
Dependencies correctly reflect that SSO service depends on tenant repository, user service, Keycloak client, and config
Each subtask has explicit boundaries (in-scope/out-of-scope), completion criteria, and related design decisions
Subtask constraints include concrete details (file paths, data formats, interface signatures) — not just one-line summaries like 'preserve backward compatibility'
Subtasks include Source references section pointing to relevant sections from system-analysis.md and implementation-design.md
Decomposition critic subagent was spawned and returned DECOMPOSITION_APPROVED
Coverage reviewer subagent was spawned and returned COVERAGE_OK with at least medium confidence
Coverage matrix maps all 7 acceptance criteria from agreed-task-model.md to specific subtasks

evals.prompt: Run Stage 5 implementation decomposition on the dashboard performance optimization task. Stage 4 is complete. Decompose the full-stack optimization into execution-ready subtasks suitable for parallel execution by multiple agents.
evals.expected_output: Execution backlog with 5-7 subtasks covering: ClickHouse bug fix, query optimization, cache creation, backend parallelization, frontend utilities (hook + downsampling), frontend component refactoring (Overview + Chart + ChartGrid). Backend and frontend work identified as parallelizable. Conflict zones identified for service.go integration. Coverage matrix validates all acceptance criteria.

assertions.text: All 4 decomposition artifacts created with all required template sections
Backend subtasks (ClickHouse fix, cache, parallelization) and frontend subtasks (hook, downsampling, component refactoring) are separated and can run in parallel across waves
Retention cohort bug fix (queries.go:67-85) is an independent subtask or part of a foundation subtask with no blocking dependencies
Cache subtask (chart_cache.go) is correctly identified as a dependency for the service parallelization subtask
Frontend useChartQuery hook is correctly identified as a dependency for Overview.tsx refactoring
Conflict zones identified where service.go integration depends on both cache and ClickHouse changes
Coverage reviewer returns COVERAGE_OK — all 7 acceptance criteria mapped
Execution waves maximize parallelism: independent backend and frontend streams can run simultaneously
Subtask constraints include concrete details (specific file paths, function signatures, data formats) extracted from system-analysis.md and implementation-design.md
Subtasks include Source references section with pointers to relevant analysis and design sections

evals.prompt: Run Stage 5 implementation decomposition. Here are the design artifacts from Stage 4.
evals.expected_output: Stage 5 should reject the input and send it back to Stage 4. The implementation design is incomplete (vague, no concrete changes), no stage-4-handoff.md exists, no agreed-task-model.md exists, and the change map has no actual content.

assertions.text: Missing stage-4-handoff.md is detected and flagged
Incomplete implementation design is recognized (vague approach, no concrete changes, no decisions)
User is told to return to Stage 4 to complete the design
No decomposition artifacts (execution-backlog.md, coverage-matrix.md) are created from incomplete inputs
Response mentions that agreed-task-model.md is missing, limiting coverage validation
